topic: Automated home-cage monitoring for mouse behavior and social interaction
review_question: What automated home-cage monitoring systems have been used to measure mouse behavior, especially social interaction, activity, preference, dominance, feeding, drinking, cognition, or welfare-related outcomes, and what results have these studies reported?
inclusion_criteria: Studies mice or rats using automated home-cage, RFID, video, sensor, or semi-natural monitoring
Reports behavioral, social, preference, dominance, activity, feeding, drinking, cognition, pain, welfare, or interaction outcomes
exclusion_criteria: Review, editorial, protocol-only, or commentary article
Non-rodent study unless it directly informs an automated monitoring method
Unavailable abstract and unavailable full text
extraction_detail_instructions: Write a detailed 6-10 sentence paper summary when abstract or full-text metadata is available.
Separate methods, outcomes, main results, conclusions, limitations, and review relevance.
Capture animal species, strain, sex, age, sample size, monitoring duration, apparatus/system name, sensors, behavioral variables, and experimental condition whenever reported.
Extract numerical findings, p-values, effect directions, units, time windows, and confidence intervals whenever reported.
Clearly mark fields as missing when the abstract or metadata does not provide the information.
Do not invent details. If only title/abstract metadata is available, state that the extraction is abstract-limited.
custom_extraction_questions: What automated home-cage, RFID, video, sensor, or monitoring system was used?
What behavior categories were measured, especially social interaction, activity, preference, dominance, feeding, drinking, cognition, pain, or welfare?
What animal model, strain, sex, age, sample size, and group structure were used?
What was the monitoring duration and experimental timeline?
What were the main behavioral findings and were numerical results reported?
How directly does this paper answer the review question?
What important information is missing from the abstract or metadata?
